title: Aureline mixed-version negotiation envelope
description: Generic negotiation envelope for named distributed or file/schema boundaries (launcher and local sidecars, desktop and CLI talking to a remote agent, desktop/CLI/browser talking to a managed control plane, extension host and SDK, schema or state bundle producer and consumer, provider-linked packet lanes, audit/event producers and consumers, and CLI/export boundaries). A single envelope shape binds capability and version fields for producer and consumer, a named skew-window class, an upgrade-order and rollback-order reference, fail-closed state, repair hints, and per-boundary reserved fields so qualification, compatibility, and release-evidence tooling can consume the same inspectable record instead of inventing prose-only compatibility identity. Later reports extend the envelope by reference; they do not rename boundary rows or collapse mixed-version status into ad hoc spreadsheets.

schema_version.description: Envelope schema version. Required so downstream consumers can reject unknown envelope epochs mechanically instead of silently widening trust.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for this envelope family. Must be the literal record_kind above so envelopes cannot be confused with other compat records.

envelope_id.description: Stable envelope instance id. Cited by compatibility reports, claim manifests, release-evidence packets, and support bundles.

boundary_family.description: Named boundary family the envelope speaks for. Reserved vocabulary; new families land by extending the enum, not by free-text aliasing.

boundary_row_ref.description: Stable compat_row id from artifacts/compat/qualification_matrix_seed.yaml. The envelope extends one row rather than inventing a parallel identity.

artifact_or_protocol_boundary_label.description: Stable boundary label copied from the qualification matrix.

skew_window_declaration_ref.description: Stable skew-window id from artifacts/compat/skew_windows.yaml. Points at the machine-readable declaration carrying supported window, upgrade order, rollback order, downgrade behavior, and unsupported-state behavior.

claimed_deployment_profiles.description: Deployment profiles the envelope currently claims. Must overlap the claimed profiles of the referenced compat_row.

producer.description: Producer side of the boundary (launcher, control surface, extension host, producer-side schema emitter, event emitter, CLI export writer, and so on).

consumer.description: Consumer side of the boundary (local sidecar, remote agent, managed control plane, extension runtime, schema/state consumer, provider-linked packet reader, audit or event consumer, CLI export reader).

negotiation.description: Outcome of the capability intersection, not an optimistic superset.

skew_window.description: Snapshot of the currently claimed skew window, class, and window status for this envelope instance.

upgrade_order.description: Upgrade-order declaration and compliance posture. Violations fail closed or degrade with a visible reason; they are never silent.

rollback_order.description: Rollback-order declaration and compliance posture so rollback paths stay symmetric with upgrade order.

downgrade_behavior.description: Declared downgrade behavior, including whether downgrade is supported, required, or explicitly unsupported.

fail_closed_state.description: Fail-closed state when the envelope resolves outside window, outside upgrade order, or against an unsupported combination. Every envelope carries this field so unsupported combinations never render as undefined behavior.

unsupported_state_behavior.description: Declared behavior for unsupported combinations on this boundary. Distinct from fail_closed_state so tooling can distinguish the current instance posture from the boundary's always-on rule.

repair_hints.description: Ordered repair hints the client or operator may attempt. Rendered in desktop UI, CLI diagnostics, admin docs, and support exports from the same contract.

reserved_boundary_fields.description: Per-boundary reserved fields so envelopes can carry boundary-specific negotiation detail (for example contract_version for launcher/sidecars, WIT ABI for extension host, tenancy/region epoch for managed control plane) without minting parallel envelope shapes.

captured_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when this envelope instance was captured.

notes.description: Short free-form reviewer note. Never the source of truth for support class, skew window, upgrade order, or repair path.

boundary_family.description: Reserved boundary-family vocabulary. Covers the named distributed and file/schema boundaries called out by the Aureline architecture and design docs.

contract_version.description: Machine-readable contract version. Semver, schema epoch, WIT ABI version, manifest revision, or equivalent stable revision string.

schema_epoch.description: Schema epoch for producers or consumers that version via epoch rather than semver.

min_supported_contract_version.description: Minimum contract version this participant accepts.

max_tested_contract_version.description: Maximum contract version this participant has been exercised against. May equal contract_version when only the current version is claimed.

capability_set.description: Declared capability tokens this participant advertises. Capability negotiation chooses the intersection.

build_identity_ref.description: Exact-build identity ref where the participant is a compiled artifact. Null where the participant is a contract (schema, envelope) rather than a binary.

toolchain_epoch.description: Toolchain-manifest epoch for participants that carry a toolchain (for example remote agent or extension runtime).

tenancy_region_epoch.description: Tenancy or region epoch for managed-control-plane participants. Null elsewhere.

sdk_support_window_ref.description: Published SDK support window ref for extension-host/SDK participants. Null elsewhere.

permission_vocabulary_version.description: Permission-vocabulary version for extension-host participants. Null elsewhere.

provider_service_family_ref.description: Provider-service-family ref for provider-linked packet participants. Null elsewhere.

negotiation_outcome.description: Closed vocabulary for the negotiation outcome. 'compatible' means the intersection covers all claimed capabilities. 'compatible_with_partial_feature_narrowing' means the intersection is non-empty but narrower than either side's advertised set. The refused_* verdicts all render as fail-closed to the user with an attributed reason.

negotiated_capability_set.description: Capability tokens actually in force for this envelope instance. Empty array is allowed only when outcome is a refused_* verdict.

dropped_capabilities.description: Capabilities that were advertised by one side but dropped by the intersection. Every drop records a typed reason class.

selected_contract_version.description: Contract version the intersection settles on. For fail-closed outcomes this is the version the consumer or producer refused.

reason.description: Visible reason. Required for every outcome, including 'compatible', so release evidence and support bundles can render one consistent reason line.

skew_window_status.description: Current skew-window status. Covers the supported/best_effort/untested/unsupported buckets from the version-skew register plus the outside_window_posture vocabulary.

window_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the currently claimed skew window.

current_skew_case_ref.description: Matching skew_case_id from the version-skew register when the envelope resolves to a named case.

upgrade_order_compliance.description: Upgrade-order compliance verdict. 'not_applicable_coordinated_set_only' is reserved for boundaries that only promote the whole artifact graph together.

declared_order.description: Ordered participant component families that must be upgraded in the listed order. A single entry means the boundary promotes one coordinated artifact set.

violation_note.description: Required note when compliance is a violated_* verdict; null otherwise.

declared_order.description: Ordered participant component families that must roll back in the listed order. Inverse of the upgrade_order where applicable; boundaries that must roll back together keep one entry.

violation_note.description: Required note when compliance is 'violated'; null otherwise.

support_class.description: Declared downgrade behavior class. Must match the declaration in artifacts/compat/skew_windows.yaml.

state_preservation_note.description: How durable state is preserved or refused on downgrade. Null when the boundary does not carry durable state.

active.description: True when fail-closed is currently in force for this envelope instance.

posture.description: Posture in force. 'none' is valid only when active is false.

visible_reason.description: Attributed reason the boundary is failing closed. Rendered verbatim in desktop UI, CLI output, admin docs, and support exports.

state_class.description: Current state class for this envelope instance.

contract_rule.description: The contractual rule the boundary applies when the current combination is unsupported. Must match the rule in artifacts/compat/skew_windows.yaml for this boundary.

repair_hint_kind.description: Closed repair-hint vocabulary. Every hint is one of these classes so repair paths are machine-readable.

reversible.description: True when the hint is safely reversible.

reserved_boundary_fields.description: At most one per-boundary-family block should be populated; the block that matches boundary_family carries the reserved fields for that family. Unpopulated blocks may be omitted.
